text: The internet is freaking out, and for
good reason, because this might be the
biggest news of all time. A few weeks
back, the presidents of China and Russia
were caught on a hot mic talking about
how people this century might live to
see 150 thanks to advancements in
biotechnology. Just a few weeks later,
and this study was published in Cell,
where Chinese scientists were able to
successfully reverse key signs of aging
in monkeys using genetically altered
human stem cells. Over a 44-week trial,
elderly monkeys received a bi-weekly IV
infusion of a special type of stem cell
called SRC that revealed a multi-system
rejuvenation across 10 major
physiological systems and 61 tissue
types. Treated monkeys showed improved
memory, healthier brain structures,
stronger bones, and restored
reproductive functions. And tissue
analysis revealed reduced brain atrophy,
inflammation, osteoporosis, fibrosis,
and liquid buildup. And half of the
tissues tested showed a reversal of
aging related gene expressions to a
younger
